summary: The page shell reserves container slots for the rendered-output region and the error region; the output/error wiring populates and toggles visibility of those containers. The shell provides distinct visual treatments so the two regions are readable and distinguishable.

slots.name: output_region
slots.ownership: Shell owns the container element and its surrounding layout/heading. Output/error wiring owns the container's children (the rendered math DOM produced by the renderer) and may replace those children on every update.

stable_attributes.data_role: latex-output (so tests and other sections can locate it)

slots.name: error_region
slots.ownership: Shell owns the container element and base styling (color/border/role). Output/error wiring owns the visible/hidden state and the text content of the message inside.

stable_attributes.aria: role='alert' or aria-live='polite' on the error region (shell provides; output/error wiring may rely on it).

interface.visual_distinction_contract: Shell CSS guarantees that output_region and error_region are visually distinct from each other and from the input region (e.g., distinct background, border, or text color). Output/error wiring relies on this and does not need to set inline error styling.

data_passed: Output (shell -> output/error wiring): two stable container DOM element references (or selectors) for output_region and error_region.
No runtime data flows through this contract — it is a structural/DOM contract only.
non_goals: Shell does not call the renderer or read input.
Output/error wiring does not set page-level layout.
Shell does not decide error message text.
